todaysEvents.summary: Iran suspends peace talks; US hits Iranian radar/drone installations; Iran targets US base in Kuwait (Jun 1-2)
todaysEvents.delta: Up: Iran suspends peace talks (Jun 1, The New Arab, Anadolu) — major reversal of deal momentum. US hit Iranian radar and drone installations; Iran retaliated targeting US military base in Kuwait (Semafor, Jun 1-2). Trump: 'it will all work out well in the end'. Israel-Hezbollah halt hostilities agreed (Al Jazeera Jun 1) — but Iran warns Lebanon/Gaza strikes threaten ceasefire talks. Barclays warns oil risks 'skewed higher'. Ships sailing dark through Hormuz — remains largely closed.

todaysEvents.summary: Oil closes up more than 4% on halt in US-Iran talks, risk of blockades (Jun 1, Reuters)
todaysEvents.delta: Up: Brent closed up more than 4% on Monday June 1 (Reuters) — Iran suspends talks + direct US-Iran fire exchange drive risk repricing. Brent current $94.53 (OilPrice.com Jun 2). WTI $91.67. Gold $4,482.90 (Kitco Jun 1 close). Market reverses May's -19% trajectory on sudden escalatory shift.

todaysEvents.summary: Trump: Israel and Hezbollah agree to halt hostilities; Lebanon invasion pause (Jun 1, Al Jazeera)
todaysEvents.delta: Neutral: Trump announces Israel and Hezbollah agreed to halt hostilities (Jun 1, Al Jazeera). Lebanon invasion paused. But Iran warns that continued Israeli attacks in Lebanon and Gaza threaten the broader US ceasefire talks — demands 'ceasefire on all fronts'. Ben-Gvir says 'time to say no to Trump' and continue striking Hezbollah. Partial de-escalation on one front may not translate to Iran deal progress.

scenarios.label: Base
scenarios.description: Iran suspended peace talks (Jun 1) — major reversal of MOU momentum. US-Iran direct military exchange: US hits Iranian radar/drone installations; Iran targets Kuwait base. Trump: 'it will all work out well in the end'. Israel-Hezbollah halt agreed but Iran warns Lebanon/Gaza strikes threaten talks. 60-day MOU framework exists (Axios, CNBC) but talks suspension + military exchange widen gap materially. Base probability reduced from 28% to 15% — path to deal now requires talks to resume first. If talks resume and deal signs: Brent retreats toward $80-85/bbl; P&I restoration enables commercial traffic; Ras Laffan restart ~late Aug 2026.

scenarios.label: Stress
scenarios.description: Talks suspended but Trump insists peace talks continuing. Israel-Hezbollah halt removes one complicating front. Military exchange contained (US hit radar/drone, Iran hit Kuwait base — no structural escalation to theatre war). Partial normalisation path: talks resume within days, MOU framework revived but nuclear sequencing + Strait sovereignty + frozen assets impasse drags. Phase 1 Hormuz partial reopening while nuclear dispute delays Phase 2; mine-clearing ~6 months delays full commercial recovery; P&I clubs restore conditionally; oil stabilises $85-105/bbl. TTF €42-50/MWh; JKM $15-20/MMBtu; iTraxx Asia IG 165-200bp. Ras Laffan restart not before late Aug 2026 even on deal. Dominant scenario (50%) — talks suspension temporary, not collapse.

scenarios.label: Tail
scenarios.description: Talks suspended (Jun 1) + direct US-Iran military exchange (US hits radar/drone, Iran hits Kuwait base) signals both sides escalating. Iran warns Lebanon/Gaza strikes threaten talks — tying fronts together makes de-escalation harder. Trump demands incompatible with Iran's frozen assets + nuclear sovereignty gap. MOU framework now in serious jeopardy — any collapse before talks resume triggers full military escalation. Brent spikes $125-165/bbl; Kharg Island seizure or GCC production targeting; Malaysia July-August supply crisis; iTraxx spikes to 230-260bp. Tail (35%) — up from 27% on talks suspension + direct military exchange.
